name: Grab Holdings
sector: SEA super-app — mobility, deliveries, digital financial services

evidence.title: Grab posts first full-year adjusted EBITDA profit
On-demand GMV +18% YoY; mobility back above pre-COVID
GrabFin lending book scaling; digibank losses narrowing
Incentive spend ticks up to defend share vs foodpanda exit markets

earnings_video.title: Grab Holdings Q4 FY25 Earnings Call
earnings_video.quote: We delivered our first full year of positive adjusted EBITDA, and we are now focused on durable, profitable growth and capital returns.

analyst_steps.query: Grab Q4 FY25 adjusted EBITDA segment profitability
analyst_steps.observation: Deliveries + mobility both segment-EBITDA positive; group FCF inflecting
Mgmt signals disciplined incentives; take-rate up ~40bps QoQ
Fintech adj EBITDA loss narrowed ~35% YoY; deposit growth strong

thesis.summary: Grab has crossed into group profitability with both core segments EBITDA-positive and fintech losses shrinking fast. With ~$5.5B net cash and a fresh buyback, the story shifts from burn to capital return. Valuation is reasonable if incentive discipline holds.
risks: Re-acceleration of incentive spend if competition returns in key markets
Digital bank (GXBank/Superbank) losses if deposit costs rise
Regulatory pressure on gig-worker classification across SEA
